Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

◆ augment_ng_memories()

void BucketGraph::augment_ng_memories ( std::vector< double > & solution,
std::vector< Path > & paths,
bool aggressive,
int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 328 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

330 {
331 // Pre-allocate with estimated size to avoid reallocations
332 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> cycles;
333 cycles.reserve(paths.size() /
334 4); // Estimate 25% of paths might have cycles
335
336 // Detect cycles in fractional paths
337 for (size_t col = 0; col < paths.size(); ++col) {
338 const double epsilon = 1e-2;
339 if (solution[col] <= epsilon || solution[col] >= 1 - epsilon) continue;
340
341 const auto &path = paths[col];
342 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<uint16_t, int> visited_clients;
343 visited_clients.reserve(path.size());
344
345 for (size_t i = 0; i < path.size(); ++i) {
346 const uint16_t client = static_cast<uint16_t>(path[i]);
347 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) continue;
348
349 auto it = visited_clients.find(client);
350 if (it != visited_clients.end()) {
351 // Cycle found - extract it
352 std::vector<uint16_t> cycle;
353 cycle.reserve(i - it->second + 1);
354
355 for (size_t j = it->second; j <= i; ++j) {
356 cycle.push_back(static_cast<uint16_t>(path[j]));
357 }
358
359 cycles.push_back(cycle);
360 break;
361 }
362 visited_clients[client] = i;
363 }
364 }
365
366 if (cycles.empty()) return;
367
368 // Sort cycles by size
369 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(),
370 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.size() < b.size(); });
371
372 // Process cycles
373 int forbidden_count = 0;
374
375 // Lambda for counting bits in neighborhoods
376 auto count_neighborhood_bits = [this, eta_max](uint16_t node) -> int {
377 int count = 0;
378 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
379 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment);
380 if (count >= eta_max) return count;
381 }
382 return count;
383 };
384
385 for (const auto &cycle : cycles) {
386 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
387
388 // Check if cycle can be forbidden
389 bool can_forbid = true;
390 for (const uint16_t node : cycle) {
391 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
392 can_forbid = false;
393 break;
394 }
395 }
396
397 if (can_forbid && (cycle.size() <= eta1 || forbidden_count < eta2)) {
398 // Convert uint16_t vector to int vector for forbidCycle
399 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
400 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
401 if (++forbidden_count >= eta2) break;
402 }
403 }
404}

447 {
448 // Stage-specific initializations

450 heuristic_fixing<S>();
451 } else if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
452 if (first_reset) {
453 reset_fixed();
454 first_reset = false;
455 }
456#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
457 if (options.bucket_fixing) {
458 bucket_fixing<S>();
459 }
460#endif
461 }
462
463 reset_pool();
464 common_initialization();
465
466 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size());
467 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size());
468
469 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
470 run_labeling_algorithms<S, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
471 } else {
472 forward_cbar =
473 labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, S, Full::Partial>();
474 }
475
476 auto best_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
477 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
478 if (check_feasibility(fw_best_label, bw_best_label)) {
479 best_label = compute_label<S>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
480 } else {
481 best_label->cost = 0.0;
482 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
483 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
484 }
485 } else {
486 best_label->cost = best_label->real_cost;
487 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
488 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
489 }
490 merged_labels.push_back(best_label);
491
492 const size_t n_segments = (fw_buckets_size + 63) / 64;
493 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments);
494
495 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
496 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < fw_buckets_size; ++bucket) {
497 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
498
499 // if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
500 // non_dominated_labels_per_bucket += bucket_labels.size();
501 // }
502
503 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
504 if (L->is_dominated) {
505 continue;
506 }
507 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket++;
508 const auto &to_arcs =
509 nodes[L->node_id].get_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
510
511 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
512 const int to_node = arc.to;
513
514 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
515 // if (fixed_arcs[L->node_id][to_node] == 1) {
516 if (is_arc_fixed(L->node_id, to_node)) {
517 continue;
518 }
519 }
520
521 auto extended_labels =
522 Extend<Direction::Forward, S, ArcType::Node,
523 Mutability::Const, Full::Reverse>(L, arc);
524 for (Label *L_prime : extended_labels) {
525 int bucket_to_process =
526 L_prime->vertex; // Create mutable copy
527 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
528 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
529 ConcatenateLabel<S, SYM>(L, bucket_to_process, best_cost,
530 Bvisited);
531 }
532 }
533 }
534 }
535
536 pdqsort(merged_labels.begin(), merged_labels.end(),
537 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
538
539#ifdef SCHRODINGER
540 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
541 std::vector<Path> paths;
542 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();
543 const int end_idx = std::min(N_ADD + N_ADD, labels_size);
544
545 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);
546 for (int i = N_ADD; i < end_idx; ++i) {
547 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
548 continue;
549 }
550 paths.emplace_back(merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered,
551 merged_labels[i]->real_cost);
552 }
553
554 sPool.add_paths(paths);
555 sPool.iterate();
556 }
557#endif
558
559#ifdef RIH
560
561 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
562 std::vector<Label *> top_labels;
563 top_labels.reserve(N_ADD);
564
565 const int n_candidates =
566 std::min(N_ADD, static_cast<int>(merged_labels.size()));
567 for (int i = 0; i < n_candidates; ++i) {
568 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
569 continue;
570 }
571 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
572 }
573 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
574 }
575#endif
576
577 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
578 return merged_labels;
579}

void bucket_fixing()

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 971 of file BucketUtils.h.

971 {
972 // Stage 4 bucket arc fixing
973 if (!fixed) {
974 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE \033[0m");
975 fmt::print("\n");
976 fixed = true;
977 common_initialization();
978
979 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(
980 fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
981 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(
982 bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
983
984 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar,
985 backward_cbar);
986
987 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
988
989 // check if gap is -inf and early exit, due to IPM
990 if (gap < 0) {
991 fmt::print(
992 "\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO "
993 "GAP\033[0m");
994 fmt::print("\n");
995 // print the gap
996 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
997 return;
998 }
999 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
1000 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
1001
1002 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
1003
1004 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1005 work, bi_sched,
1006 SECTION {
1007 // Section 1: Forward direction
1008 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
1009 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
1010 },
1011 SECTION {
1012 // Section 2: Backward direction
1013 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
1014 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
1015 });
1016
1017 generate_arcs();
1018 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m");
1019 fmt::print("\n");
1020 just_fixed = true;
1021 return;
1022 }
1023 just_fixed = false;
1024}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

Definition at line 176 of file BucketJump.h.

176 {
177 // Assign forward or backward buckets, adjacency lists, and fixed buckets
178 // based on direction
179 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
180 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
181 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
182 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
183 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
184 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
185 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
186 // Reset fixed_buckets
187 auto n_buckets = buckets_size;
188
189 // for (auto &fb : fixed_buckets) { std::fill(fb.begin(), fb.end(), 0); }
190 // set fixed_buckets_bitmap all to 0
191 fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fixed_buckets_bitmap.size(), 0);
192
193 using ArcMap =
194 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>,
195 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
196 arc_map_hash>;
197
198 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
199 int removed_arcs = 0;
200
201 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) {
202 return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b);
203 };
204
205 // Function to process jump arcs
206 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
207 const auto &jump_arcs = buckets[b].template get_jump_arcs<D>();
208 if (!jump_arcs.empty()) {
209 const size_t n_segments = buckets_size / 64 + 1;
210 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
211 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
212 for (const auto &a : jump_arcs) {
213 auto increment = a.resource_increment;
214
215 // auto arc_key =
216 // std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket),
217 // b);
218 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket, b);
219 int b_opposite =
220 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.jump_bucket, increment);
221
222 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
223
224 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
225 Bidi_map.insert(b);
226 if (!Bvisited.empty()) {
227 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
228 Bvisited.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
229 }
230 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
231 Bvisited);
232 }
233 }
234 }
235 };
236
237 // Function to process bucket arcs
238 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
239 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
240 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
241
242 const size_t n_segments = buckets_size / 64 + 1;
243 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
244
245 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
246 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0);
247 // print increment.size
248 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
249 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
250 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
251 } else {
252 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
253 }
254 }
255 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id,
256 buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
257 int b_opposite =
258 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
259 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
260
261 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
262 Bidi_map.insert(b);
263 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
264 Bvisited.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
265 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
266 Bvisited);
267 }
268
269 // Check for opposite direction arcs
270 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(arc_key);
271 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
272 auto &Bidi_map_opposite = it->second;
273 bool contains = std::any_of(
274 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].begin(),
275 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].end(), [&](int b_prime) {
276 return Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_prime) !=
277 Bidi_map_opposite.end();
278 });
279
280 if (!contains && Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_opposite) ==
281 Bidi_map_opposite.end()) {
282 size_t bit_pos = a.from_bucket * n_buckets + a.to_bucket;
283 fixed_buckets_bitmap[bit_pos / 64] |=
284 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
285
286 ++removed_arcs;
287 }
288 }
289 }
290 };
291
292 // Process each bucket
293 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
294 const auto &node_arcs =
295 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
296
297 // Process node arcs
298 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
299 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
300 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
301
302 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
303 Bidi_map.insert(b);
304 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
305 auto neighbor_key =
306 std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
307 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key);
308 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
309 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
310 }
311 }
312 }
313 }
314
315 // Process jump and bucket arcs using separate functions
316 process_jump_arcs(b);
317 process_bucket_arcs(b);
318 }
319
320 // Print the number of arcs removed
321 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
322 print_info("[Fw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
323 } else {
324 print_info("[Bw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
325 }
326}

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 267 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

267 {
268 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
269
270 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmaps as vectors of uint64_t for
271 // forward and backward neighborhoods
272 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Forward neighborhood
273
274 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
275 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>>
276 forward_distances; // Distances for forward neighbors
277
278 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
279 if (i != j) {
280 // Forward distance (i -> j)
281 double forward_distance = getcij(i, j);
282 forward_distances.push_back({forward_distance, j});
283 }
284 }
285
286 // Sort distances to find the closest nodes
287 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end());
288
289 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap vector for node i (forward and
290 // backward)
291 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
292 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments,
293 0); // Resizing for forward bitmap
294
295 // Include the node itself in both forward and backward
296 // neighborhoods
297 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
298 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
299 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment_self] |=
300 (1ULL << bit_position_self); // Forward
301
302 // Map the top 'num_closest' closest nodes for forward and set them
303 // in the backward neighborhoods
304 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size();
305 ++k) {
306 size_t node_index = forward_distances[k].second;
307
308 // Determine the segment and the bit within the segment for the
309 // node_index (forward)
310 size_t segment = node_index >> 6;
311 size_t bit_position = node_index & 63;
312 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |=
313 (1ULL << bit_position); // Forward neighbor
314 }
315 }
316}

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 840 of file BucketGraph.h.

841 {
842 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
843
844 // Cache resources and node data
845 const auto &fw_resources = fw_label->resources;
846 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
847 const struct VRPNode &VRPNode = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
848
849 // Time feasibility check
850 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
851 if (options.resources[r] != "time") {
852 continue;
853 }
854 const auto time_fw = fw_resources[r];
855 const auto time_bw = bw_resources[r];
856 const auto travel_time =
857 getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
858 if (time_fw + travel_time + VRPNode.duration > time_bw) {
859 return false;
860 }
861 }
862
863 // Resource feasibility check (if applicable)
864 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
865 for (size_t i = 1; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
866 const auto resource_fw = fw_resources[i];
867 const auto resource_bw = bw_resources[i];
868 const auto demand = VRPNode.demand;
869
870 if (resource_fw + demand > resource_bw) {
871 return false;
872 }
873 }
874 }
875
876 return true;
877 }

1319 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
1320
1321 auto &warm_labels = assign_buckets<D>(fw_warm_labels, bw_warm_labels);
1322 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1323 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1324 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
1325 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1326
1327 if (merged_labels.size() > 0 && options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1328 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
1329 warm_labels.clear();
1330 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
1331 for (auto bucket = 0; bucket < buckets_size; ++bucket) {
1332 if (auto *label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label()) {
1333 warm_labels.push_back(label);
1334 }
1335 }
1336 }
1337
1338 // Initialize vectors with exact sizes once
1339 c_bar.resize(buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1340 // print size of c_bar
1341
1342 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1343 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(buckets_size + 1, 0);
1344 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
1345 }
1346
1347 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[0];
1348
1349 for (size_t b = 0; b < buckets_size; b++) {
1350 buckets[b].clear();
1351 }
1352
1353 // Calculate intervals once
1354 for (size_t r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
1355 base_intervals[r] =
1356 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / bucket_splits[VRPNode.id];
1357 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
1358 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
1359 }
1360
1361 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1362 pdqsort(
1363 warm_labels.begin(), warm_labels.end(),
1364 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
1365
1366 std::vector<Label *> processed_labels;
1367 const size_t process_size = std::min(
1368 warm_labels.size(), static_cast<size_t>(options.n_warm_start));
1369 processed_labels.reserve(process_size);
1370
1371 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
1372 auto label = warm_labels[i];
1373 if (!label->fresh) continue;
1374
1375 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(label, true);
1376 if (new_label != nullptr) {
1377 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_label(new_label);
1378 processed_labels.push_back(new_label);
1379 }
1380 }
1381 warm_labels = std::move(processed_labels);
1382 }
1383
1384 // Initialize intervals
1385 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
1386 int offset = 0;
1387
1388 // Lambda for interval combination generation
1389 auto generate_combinations = [&](bool is_forward, int &offset) {
1390 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1391 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
1392 const auto depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
1393 const int calculated_index_base =
1394 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
1395 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
1396
1397 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
1398 std::function<void(int)> process_intervals = [&](int depth) {
1399 if (depth == num_intervals) {
1400 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
1401 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
1402
1403 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1404 interval_bounds[r] =
1405 is_forward
1406 ? interval_starts[r] +
1407 current_pos[r] *
1408 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
1409 : interval_ends[r] -
1410 current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(
1411 base_intervals[r]);
1412 }
1413
1414 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
1415 depot_id);
1416 depot->is_extended = false;
1417 depot->nodes_covered.push_back(depot_id);
1418 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
1419 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
1420 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
1421 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
1422 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
1423
1424 offset++;
1425 return;
1426 }
1427
1428 for (int k = 0; k < bucket_splits[depot_id]; ++k) {
1429 current_pos[depth] = k;
1430 process_intervals(depth + 1);
1431 }
1432 };
1433
1434 process_intervals(0);
1435 };
1436
1437 // Process forward and backward directions
1438 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1439 generate_combinations(true, offset);
1440 } else {
1441 generate_combinations(false, offset);
1442 }
1443}

◆ compute_mono_label()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

Computes the mono label for the BucketGraph.

This function computes the mono label for the BucketGraph by acquiring a new label from the label pool and setting the cost and real cost values from the given label L. It then calculates the number of nodes covered by the label and its ancestors, reserves space for the nodes covered, and inserts the nodes from the

1135 {

1137 double red_cost = 0.0;

1141
1142 if (fw) {
1143 if (main_resource > q_star_value) {
1144 return nullptr;
1145 }
1146 } else {
1147 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) {
1148 return nullptr;
1149 }
1150 }
1151
1152 // Initialize an empty SRCmap for the current label
1153 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->size(), 0.0);
1154
1155 int last_node = -1;
1156 if (L->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
1157 return nullptr;
1158 }
1159 auto new_label = new Label();
1160
1161 // Traverse through the label nodes from current to root
1162 for (auto node_id : L->nodes_covered) {
1164 // NG-route feasibility check
1167 if (is_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
1168 return nullptr;
1169 }
1170
1171 size_t limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size();
1172 for (size_t i = 0; i < limit; ++i) {
1173 uint64_t current_visited =
1174 new_label->visited_bitmap[i]; // Get visited nodes
1175 // in the current
1176 // segment
1177
1178 if (!current_visited)
1179 continue; // Skip if no nodes were visited in
1180 // this segment
1181
1182 uint64_t neighborhood_mask =
1183 neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i]; // Get neighborhood mask
1184 // for the current node
1185 uint64_t bits_to_clear =
1186 current_visited & neighborhood_mask; // Determine which
1187 // bits to clear
1188
1189 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
1190 bits_to_clear; // Clear irrelevant visited
1191 // nodes
1192 }
1193 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap,
1194 node_id); // Mark the new node as visited
1195
1196 if (last_node != -1) {
1197 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
1198 real_cost += cij_cost;
1199 red_cost += cij_cost;
1200 }
1201
1202 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
1203
1205 // SRC logic
1208 size_t segment =
1209 node_id >> 6; // Determine the segment in the bitmap
1210 size_t bit_position =
1211 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position in the segment
1212
1213 // Update the SRCmap for each cut
1214 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1215 auto &SRCDuals = cutter->SRCDuals;
1216 const uint64_t bit_mask = 1ULL << bit_position;
1217
1218 for (std::size_t idx = 0; idx < cutter->size(); ++idx) {
1219 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
1220 const auto &baseSet = cut.baseSet;
1221 const auto &baseSetorder = cut.baseSetOrder;
1222 const auto &neighbors = cut.neighbors;
1223 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
1224
1225#if defined(SRC)
1226 // Apply SRC logic to update the SRCmap
1227 const bool bitIsSet = neighbors[segment] & bit_mask;
1228
1229 auto &src_map_value = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1230 if (!bitIsSet) {
1231 src_map_value = 0.0; // Reset if bit is not set
1232 // in neighbors
1233 continue;
1234 }
1235 const bool bitIsSet2 = baseSet[segment] & bit_mask;
1236
1237 if (bitIsSet2) {
1238 auto &den = multipliers.den;
1239 src_map_value += multipliers.num[baseSetorder[node_id]];
1240 if (src_map_value >= den) {
1241 red_cost -= SRCDuals[idx]; // Apply the SRC dual
1242 // value if threshold is
1243 // exceeded
1244 src_map_value -= den; // Reset the SRC map value
1245 }
1246 }
1247#endif
1248 }
1249
1250 // Adjust for arc duals if in Stage::Four
1251#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1252 if (last_node) {
1253 auto arc_dual = arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id);
1254 red_cost -= arc_dual;
1255 }
1256#endif
1257
1258 // Adjust for branching duals if they exist
1259 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node) {
1260 red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id);
1261 }
1262
1263 // Move to the parent node and update last_node
1264 last_node = node_id;
1265 }
1266
1267 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1268 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1269 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1270 new_label->node_id = L->node_id;
1271 new_label->nodes_covered = L->nodes_covered;
1272 new_label->is_extended = false;
1273 new_label->resources = L->resources;
1274 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1275
1276 // Bucket number for the new label
1277 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1278 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
1279 new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i];
1280 }
1281 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(
1282 new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1283 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(
1284 new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1285 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1286 new_label->fresh = false;
1287
1288 return new_label;
1289 };

197 std::vector<int> phi;
198
199 // Ensure bucket_id is within valid bounds
200 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
201 auto &fixed_buckets = fw ? fw_fixed_buckets : bw_fixed_buckets;
202 auto &node_interval_trees =
203 fw ? fw_node_interval_trees : bw_node_interval_trees;
204 auto &bucket_splits = fw ? fw_bucket_splits : bw_bucket_splits;
205 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
206 if (bucket_id >= buckets.size() || bucket_id < 0) return phi;
207
208 std::vector<double> total_ranges(intervals.size());
209 std::vector<double> base_intervals(intervals.size());
210
211 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
212 total_ranges[r] =
213 R_max[r] - R_min[r]; // Ensure integer type for total range
214 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / bucket_splits[bucket_id];
215 }
216
217 // Get the node ID and current bucket
218 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
219 auto &current_bucket = buckets[bucket_id];
220
221 // Retrieve the pre-built Splay Tree for this node
222 auto &node_tree = node_interval_trees[node_id];
223
224 // Search for matching intervals using the existing Splay Tree
225 auto target = fw ? current_bucket.lb : current_bucket.ub;
226 std::vector<double> target_low = current_bucket.lb;
227 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
228 target[r] -= base_intervals[r]; // Adjust for the base intervals
229 }
230 TreeNode *found_node = node_tree.find(target);
231 if (found_node != nullptr &&
232 buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
233 // Check if the found bucket is fixed
234#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
235 auto not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(
236 bucket_id, found_node->bucket_index)
237 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(
238 bucket_id, found_node->bucket_index);
239 if (not_fixed)
240#endif
241 {
242 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
243 }
244 }
245 } else {
246 // Handle the case where R_SIZE == 1 with a simpler approach
247 int smaller = bucket_id - 1;
248
249 if (smaller >= 0 &&
250 buckets[smaller].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
251#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
252 if (fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(smaller, bucket_id)
253 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(smaller, bucket_id))
254#endif
255 {
256 phi.push_back(smaller);
257 }
258 }
259 }
260 return phi;
261}

584

589 const int L_node_id = L->node_id;
590 const auto &L_resources = L->resources;

592 const double L_cost = L->cost;
593 const size_t bitmap_size = L->visited_bitmap.size();
594
595 // Pre-compute constants for bit operations

597

602 cutter = cut_storage;
603 }

606#endif
607
608 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
609 const int current_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
610 bucket_stack.pop_back();
611
612 // Optimize bit operations for visited tracking
613 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
614 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
615 Bvisited[segment] |= bit_mask;
616
617 const int bucketLprimenode = other_buckets[current_bucket].node_id;

619

621#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
622 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
623 travel_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
624 }
625#endif
626
627 if (has_branching) {
628 travel_cost -=
629 branching_duals->getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
630 }
631
632 const double path_cost = L_cost + travel_cost;
633 const double bound = other_c_bar[current_bucket];
634
635 // Early bound check
636 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= best_cost) ||
637 (S == Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= gap)) {
638 continue;
639 }
640
641 // Process labels in current bucket
642 const auto &bucket = other_buckets[current_bucket];
643 const auto &labels = bucket.get_labels();
644
645 for (const Label *L_bw : labels) {
646 if (L_bw->is_dominated || L_bw->node_id == L_node_id ||
647 !check_feasibility(L, L_bw)) {
648 continue;
649 }
650
651 // Visited nodes overlap check
652 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three) {
653 bool has_overlap = false;
654 for (size_t i = 0; i < bitmap_size; ++i) {
655 if (L->visited_bitmap[i] & L_bw->visited_bitmap[i]) {
656 has_overlap = true;
657 break;
658 }
659 }
660 if (has_overlap) continue;
661 }
662
663 double total_cost = path_cost + L_bw->cost;
664
665 // SRC cost adjustments
666#if defined(SRC)
667 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
668 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
669 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
670 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
671 const double dual = active_cut.dual_value;
672
673 if (L->SRCmap[idx] + L_bw->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den) {
674 total_cost -= dual;
675 }
676 }
677 }
678#endif
679
680 // Cost-based filtering
681 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= best_cost) ||
682 (S == Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= gap)) {
683 continue;
684 }
685
686 // Create new merged label
687 auto pbest = compute_label<S>(L, L_bw, total_cost);
688 best_cost = pbest->cost;
689 // fmt::print("Best cost: {}\n", best_cost);
690 merged_labels.push_back(pbest);
691 }
692
693 // Process neighbor buckets
694 for (int b_prime : Phi_bw[current_bucket]) {
695 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
696 const uint64_t mask_prime = bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63];
697 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask_prime)) {
698 bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
699 }
700 }
701 }
702}
Stage::Enumerate
@ Enumerate

◆ define_buckets()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

Definition at line 116 of file BucketUtils.h.

116 {
117 std::vector<Bucket> old_buckets;
118 std::vector<bool> was_fixed;
119 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
120 auto old_size = buckets.size();
121
122 // Store the range of fixed buckets for each node pair
123 std::unordered_map<
124 int, std::unordered_map<
125 int, std::pair<std::vector<double>, std::vector<double>>>>
126 node_pair_fixed_ranges;
127
128 if (old_size > 0) {
129 old_buckets = buckets;
130 was_fixed.resize(old_size * old_size, false);
131 auto &old_bitmap =
132 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
133
134 // First pass: mark which bucket pairs were fixed
135 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
136 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
137 size_t old_bit_pos = i * old_size + j;
138 if (old_bit_pos / 64 < old_bitmap.size()) {
139 was_fixed[old_bit_pos] =
140 (old_bitmap[old_bit_pos / 64] &
141 (1ULL << (old_bit_pos % 64))) != 0;
142 }
143 }
144 }
145
146 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
147 // For each fixed bucket pair, track the range by node pair
148 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
149 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
150 if (!was_fixed[i * old_size + j]) continue;
151
152 const auto &bucket_from = old_buckets[i];
153 const auto &bucket_to = old_buckets[j];
154
155 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
156 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
157
158 // Initialize ranges for this node pair if not already done
159 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
160 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end()) {
161 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node] = {
162 std::vector<double>(num_intervals,
163 std::numeric_limits<double>::max()),
164 std::vector<double>(
165 num_intervals,
166 std::numeric_limits<double>::lowest())};
167 }
168
169 // Update the ranges for this node pair
170 auto &[min_lbs, max_ubs] =
171 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
172 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
173 min_lbs[r] = std::min(min_lbs[r], bucket_from.lb[r]);
174 max_ubs[r] = std::max(max_ubs[r], bucket_from.ub[r]);
175 }
176 }
177 }
178
179 // Debug output
180 // for (const auto &[from_node, to_nodes] : node_pair_fixed_ranges) {
181 // for (const auto &[to_node, range] : to_nodes) {
182 // fmt::print("Arc ({}, {}) has fixed range: ", from_node,
183 // to_node);
184 // for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
185 // fmt::print("({}, {}), ", range.first[r],
186 // range.second[r]);
187 // }
188 // fmt::print("\n");
189 // }
190 // }
191 }
192
193 // Clear existing buckets and bitmap
194 buckets.clear();
195 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
196 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
197 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
198
199 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
200 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
201 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
202
203 // Get references to direction-specific containers
204 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
205 auto &num_buckets_index =
206 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
207 auto &node_interval_trees =
208 assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
209 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
210 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
211
212 // Pre-allocate containers
213 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
214 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
215 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
216 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
217
218 // Lambda for interval calculations
219 auto calculate_interval = [](double lb, double ub, double base_interval,
220 int pos, int max_interval,
221 bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
222 double start, end;
223 if (is_forward) {
224 start = lb + pos * base_interval;
225 end =
226 (pos == max_interval - 1) ? ub : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
227 } else {
228 start =
229 (pos == max_interval - 1) ? lb : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
230 end = ub - pos * base_interval;
231 }
232 return {roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start), roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end)};
233 };
234
235 int cum_sum = 0;
236 int bucket_index = 0;
237
238 // Temporary vectors for interval calculations
239 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
240 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
241 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
242
243 // Process each node
244 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
245 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
246
247 // check if fw_bucket_splits with node_id exists
248 if (bucket_splits.find(VRPNode.id) == bucket_splits.end()) {
249 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
250 node_base_interval[r] =
251 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
252 }
253 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id] = intervals[0].interval;
254 } else {
255 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
256 node_base_interval[r] =
257 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / bucket_splits[VRPNode.id];
258 }
259 }
260
261 SplayTree node_tree;
262 int n_buckets = 0;
263
264 if (num_intervals == 1) {
265 // Single interval case
266 for (int j = 0; j < bucket_splits[VRPNode.id]; ++j) {
267 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
268 VRPNode.lb[0], VRPNode.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
269 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id], D == Direction::Forward);
270
271 interval_start[0] = start;
272 interval_end[0] = end;
273
274 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
275 interval_start[0] =
276 std::max(interval_start[0], VRPNode.lb[0]);
277 } else {
278 interval_end[0] = std::min(interval_end[0], VRPNode.ub[0]);
279 }
280
281 buckets.push_back(
282 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
283 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index++);
284 n_buckets++;
285 cum_sum++;
286 }
287 } else {
288 fmt::print("Multiple intervals\n");
289 // Multiple intervals case
290 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
291 do {
292 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
293 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
294 VRPNode.lb[r], VRPNode.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
295 pos[r], intervals[r].interval, D == Direction::Forward);
296
297 interval_start[r] = start;
298 interval_end[r] = end;
299
300 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
301 interval_start[r] =
302 std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
303 } else {
304 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
305 }
306 }
307
308 buckets.push_back(
309 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
310 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index++);
311 n_buckets++;
312 cum_sum++;
313
314 // Generate next combination
315 int i = 0;
316 while (i < num_intervals && ++pos[i] >= intervals[i].interval) {
317 pos[i] = 0;
318 i++;
319 }
320 if (i == num_intervals) break;
321 } while (true);
322 }
323
324 // Update node-specific data
325 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
326 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
327 node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
328 }
329
330 if (!node_pair_fixed_ranges.empty()) {
331 size_t new_size = buckets.size();
332 size_t required_bitmap_words =
333 std::max(size_t(1), ((new_size * new_size) + 63) / 64);
334
335 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
336 fixed_buckets_bitmap.resize(required_bitmap_words, 0);
337
338 for (size_t from = 0; from < new_size; ++from) {
339 const auto &bucket_from = buckets[from];
340 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
341
342 // Skip if no outgoing fixed arcs from this node
343 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges.find(from_node) ==
344 node_pair_fixed_ranges.end())
345 continue;
346
347 for (size_t to = 0; to < new_size; ++to) {
348 const auto &bucket_to = buckets[to];
349 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
350
351 // Skip if this arc wasn't fixed before
352 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
353 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end())
354 continue;
355
356 const auto &range = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
357
358 // Check if both buckets are within their fixed ranges
359 bool is_in_range = true;
360 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
361 if (bucket_from.lb[r] < range.first[r] ||
362 bucket_from.ub[r] > range.second[r]) {
363 is_in_range = false;
364 break;
365 }
366 }
367
368 if (is_in_range) {
369 size_t bit_pos = from * new_size + to;
370 size_t word_idx = bit_pos / 64;
371 if (word_idx < fixed_buckets_bitmap.size()) {
372 fixed_buckets_bitmap[word_idx] |=
373 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
374 }
375 }
376 }
377 }
378 }
379
380 buckets_size = buckets.size();
381}

397 {
398 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
399
400 // Initialize elementarity sets
401 elementarity_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for elementarity sets
402
403 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
404 // Each customer should appear in its own elementarity
405 // set
406 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
407 elementarity_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
408
409 // Mark the node itself in the elementarity set
410 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
411 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
412 elementarity_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
413 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
int & stat_n_dom )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

Definition at line 962 of file BucketSolve.h.

965 {
966 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
967 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
968 const auto &uf = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_find, bw_union_find);
969 const auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_ordered_sccs, bw_ordered_sccs);
970 auto &union_cache = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_cache, bw_union_cache);
971
972 // Cache frequently used values
973 const int b_L = L->vertex;
974 const double label_cost = L->cost;
975 const size_t res_size = options.resources.size();
976
977 // Stack-based implementation with dynamic size
978 static thread_local std::vector<int> stack_buffer;
979 stack_buffer.reserve(R_SIZE);
980 stack_buffer.push_back(bucket);
981
982 const auto check_dominance = [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels,
983 int &stat_n_dom) {
984#ifndef __AVX2__
985 if (labels.size() >= 32) {
986 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(L, labels, cut_storage,
987 res_size, stat_n_dom);
988 } else
989#endif
990 {
991 for (auto &existing_label : labels) {
992 if (!existing_label->is_dominated &&
993 is_dominated<D, S>(L, existing_label)) {
994 ++stat_n_dom;
995 return true;
996 }
997 }
998 return false;
999 }
1000 };
1001
1002 // Main processing loop with optimizations
1003 while (!stack_buffer.empty()) {
1004 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer.back();
1005 stack_buffer.pop_back();
1006
1007 // Optimize bit operations using pre-computed masks
1008 const size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(current_bucket) >> 6;
1009 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
1010 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
1011
1012 // Early exit check with likely hint for better branch
1013 // prediction
1014 if (label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] &&
1015 ::precedes(sccs, current_bucket, b_L, uf, union_cache)) {
1016 return false;
1017 }
1018
1019 // Check dominance only if necessary
1020 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1021 const auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1022 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance(L, check_dominance, stat_n_dom)) {
1023 return true;
1024 }
1025 }
1026
1027 // Process neighbors with vectorization hints
1028 const auto &neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1029
1030 for (auto b_prime : neighbors) {
1031 const size_t word_idx_prime = static_cast<size_t>(b_prime) >> 6;
1032 const uint64_t mask = bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63];
1033
1034 if (!(Bvisited[word_idx_prime] & mask)) {
1035 stack_buffer.push_back(b_prime);
1036 }
1037 }
1038 }
1039
1040 return false;
1041}

template<Direction D, Stage S, ArcType A, Mutability M, Full F>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::Extend ( const std::conditional_t< M==Mutability::Mut, Label *, const Label * > L_prime,
const std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Bucket, BucketArc, std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Jump, JumpArc, Arc > > & gamma,
int depth )
inlinenoexcept

Extends the label L_prime with the given BucketArc gamma.

Definition at line 586 of file BucketSolve.h.

592 {
593 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
594 new_resources.resize(options.resources.size());
595
596 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
597 auto initial_resources = L_prime->resources; // Make a copy as in original
598 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
599
600 int node_id;
601 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
602 node_id =
603 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
604 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
605 node_id = gamma.to;
606 } else {
607 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
608 node_id = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket].node_id;
609
610 // Update copy of resources for jump arcs
611 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
612 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
613 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
614 initial_resources[i] = std::max(
615 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
616 } else {
617 initial_resources[i] = std::min(
618 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
619 }
620 }
621 }
622
623 // Early rejection checks
624 if (node_id == L_prime->node_id ||
625 is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
626 return {};
627 }
628
629 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
630 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
631 // fixed_arcs[initial_node_id][node_id] == 1) ||
632 is_arc_fixed(initial_node_id, node_id)) ||
633 (D == Direction::Backward &&
634 // fixed_arcs[node_id][initial_node_id] == 1)) {
635 is_arc_fixed(node_id, initial_node_id))) {
636 return {};
637 }
638 }
639
640 // Process resources and check feasibility
641 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
642 if (!process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources, gamma,
643 nodes[node_id],
644 options.resources.size())) {
645 return {};
646 }
647 }
648
649 // Path size checks for PSTEP/TSP
650 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
651 int n_visited = 0;
652 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap) {
653 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
654 }
655
656 if (n_visited > options.max_path_size ||
657 (n_visited == options.max_path_size &&
658 node_id != options.end_depot)) {
659 return {};
660 }
661 }
662
663 const int to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
664
665 // Handle fixed buckets
666#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
667 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four && A != ArcType::Jump) {
668 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(L_prime->vertex, to_bucket)) {
669 if (depth > 1) return {};
670
671 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> label_vector;
672 label_vector.clear();
673
674 auto jump_arcs =
675 nodes[L_prime->node_id].template get_jump_arcs<D>(node_id);
676
677 for (const auto &jump_arc : jump_arcs) {
678 auto extended_labels =
679 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Jump, Mutability::Const, F>(
680 L_prime, jump_arc, depth + 1);
681 if (!extended_labels.empty()) {
682 label_vector.insert(label_vector.end(),
683 extended_labels.begin(),
684 extended_labels.end());
685 }
686 }
687 return label_vector;
688 }
689 }
690#endif
691
692#ifdef CUSTOM_COST
693 auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
694 double new_cost =
695 cost_calculator.calculate_cost(initial_cost, // InitialCost
696 distance, // Distance
697 node_id, // NodeId
698 new_resources // Resources
699 );
700#else
701 double new_cost = initial_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
702#endif
703
704 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
705
706 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
707 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
708 } else {
709 int n_visited = 0;
710 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap) {
711 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
712 }
713
714 if (n_visited > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot) {
715 new_cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id) +
716 pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
717 }
718 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) +
719 pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
720 }
721
722 // Branching duals
723 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
724 new_cost += (D == Direction::Forward)
725 ? branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
726 : branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
727 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
728 }
729
730 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
731 new_cost -= (D == Direction::Forward)
732 ? arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
733 : arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
734 })
735
736 // Handle Reverse case
737 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
738 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <=
739 q_star[options.main_resources[0]]) {
740 return {};
741 }
742 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
743 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
744 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
745 return {new_label};
746 }
747
748 // Create and initialize new label
749 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
750 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
751 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
752 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
753 new_label->real_cost =
754 L_prime->real_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
755
756 if constexpr (M == Mutability::Mut) {
757 // new_label->parent = L_prime;
758 // L_prime->children.push_back(new_label);
759 }
760
761 // Handle visited bitmap
762 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
763 // if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
764 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
765 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
766 if (current_visited) {
767 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
768 current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i];
769 }
770 }
771 // }
772 } else {
773 new_label->visited_bitmap = L_prime->visited_bitmap;
774 }
775 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
776 new_label->nodes_covered = L_prime->nodes_covered;
777 new_label->nodes_covered.push_back(node_id);
778
779#if defined(SRC)
780
781 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
782 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
783
784 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
785 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
786 const auto n_cuts = cutter->activeSize();
787
788 if (n_cuts == 0) {
789 return {new_label};
790 }
791 double total_cost_update = 0.0;
792
793 const size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
794 const size_t bit_position = node_id & 63;
795
796 // Get active cuts once
797 auto &masks = cutter->getSegmentMasks();
798 auto cut_limit_mask = masks.get_cut_limit_mask();
799 uint64_t valid_cuts = masks.get_valid_cut_mask(segment, bit_position);
800
801 valid_cuts &= cut_limit_mask; // This is just a safeguard
802
803 while (valid_cuts) {
804 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(valid_cuts);
805
806 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[cut_idx];
807 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
808
809 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[active_cut.index];
810 src_map_value += cut.p.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
811
812 if (src_map_value >= cut.p.den) {
813 src_map_value -= cut.p.den;
814 total_cost_update -= active_cut.dual_value;
815 }
816
817 valid_cuts &= (valid_cuts - 1); // Clear current bit
818 }
819
820 // Handle cuts not in neighbors
821 uint64_t to_clear =
822 (~masks.get_neighbor_mask(segment, bit_position)) & cut_limit_mask;
823 while (to_clear) {
824 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(to_clear);
825 if (cut_idx >= n_cuts) {
826 fmt::print("Warning: Invalid clear_idx {} >= n_cuts {}\n",
827 cut_idx, n_cuts);
828 break;
829 }
830
831 new_label->SRCmap[active_cuts[cut_idx].index] = 0.0;
832 to_clear &= (to_clear - 1);
833 }
834
835 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
836 }
837#endif
838
839 // Return the array (as Label**)
840 return std::vector<Label *>{new_label};
841}
RCC_MODE_BLOCK
#define RCC_MODE_BLOCK(code_block)

◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 415 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

415 {
416 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
417 int v1 = cycle[i];
418 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
419
420 // Update the bitmap to forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1
421 size_t segment = v2 >> 6;
422 size_t bit_position = v2 & 63;
423 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
424
425 if (aggressive) {
426 segment = v1 >> 6;
427 bit_position = v1 & 63;
428 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
429 }
430 }
431}

◆ generate_arcs() [1/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 772 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

772 {
773 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
774 work, bi_sched,
775 SECTION {
776 // Task for Forward Direction
777 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
778 Phi_fw.clear();
779 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
780 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) {
781 Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true);
782 }
783 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
784 },
785 SECTION {
786 // Task for Backward Direction
787 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
788 Phi_bw.clear();
789 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
790 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) {
791 Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false);
792 }
793 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
794 });
795}

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Definition at line 390 of file BucketUtils.h.

390 {
391 auto buckets_mutex = std::mutex();
392
393 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
394 fw_bucket_graph.clear();
395 } else {
396 bw_bucket_graph.clear();
397 }
398
399 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
400 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
401 auto &num_buckets_index =
402 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
403 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
404 // Pre-compute intervals for all nodes
405 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_intervals(nodes.size());
406 for (size_t node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
407 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
408 node_intervals[node_id].resize(options.resources.size());
409 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
410 node_intervals[node_id][r] =
411 (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / bucket_splits[node_id];
412 }
413 }
414
415 // Clear buckets
416 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
417 bucket.clear();
418 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward);
419 }
420
421 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
422 std::vector<double> &res_inc,
423 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> &local_arcs) {
424 const auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
425 const auto &node_interval = node_intervals[node.id];
426
427 for (const auto &arc : arcs) {
428 // print node_id
429 const auto &next_node = nodes[arc.to];
430 if (node.id == next_node.id) continue;
431
432 const auto &next_node_interval = node_intervals[arc.to];
433 const auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
434 const double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
435
436 // Pre-calculate resource increments
437 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); r++) {
438 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
439 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
440 res_inc[r] += travel_cost;
441 }
442 }
443
444 for (int j = 0; j < num_buckets[next_node.id]; ++j) {
445 const int to_bucket = j + num_buckets_index[next_node.id];
446 if (from_bucket == to_bucket ||
447 is_bucket_fixed<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket)) {
448 continue;
449 }
450
451 bool valid_arc = true;
452
453 // Resource bounds check
454 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
455 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid_arc; ++r) {
456 if (buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r] >
457 next_node.ub[r]) {
458 valid_arc = false;
459 } else {
460 double max_calc = std::max(
461 buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r],
462 next_node.lb[r]);
463 if (max_calc < buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] ||
464 max_calc >= buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] +
465 next_node_interval[r] +
466 numericutils::eps) {
467 valid_arc = false;
468 }
469 }
470 }
471 } else {
472 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid_arc; ++r) {
473 if (buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r] <
474 next_node.lb[r]) {
475 valid_arc = false;
476 } else {
477 double min_calc = std::min(
478 buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r],
479 next_node.ub[r]);
480 if (min_calc > buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] ||
481 min_calc <= buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] -
482 next_node_interval[r] -
483 numericutils::eps) {
484 valid_arc = false;
485 }
486 }
487 }
488 }
489
490 if (valid_arc) {
491 local_arcs.emplace_back(from_bucket, to_bucket);
492 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(buckets_mutex);
493 add_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc);
494 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(
495 from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc, false);
496 }
497 }
498 }
499 };
500
501 const unsigned int thread_count = std::thread::hardware_concurrency() / 2;
502 exec::static_thread_pool pool(thread_count);
503 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
504
505 std::vector<int> tasks(nodes.size());
506 std::iota(tasks.begin(), tasks.end(), 0);
507
508 const int chunk_size = 10;
509 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
510 stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
511 [this, &tasks, &num_buckets, &num_buckets_index, &add_arcs_for_node,
512 chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
513 const size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
514 const size_t end_idx =
515 std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
516
517 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
518 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> local_arcs;
519 local_arcs.reserve(chunk_size *
520 100); // Estimate average arcs per chunk
521
522 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
523 const int node_id = tasks[task_idx];
524 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
525
526 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[node.id]; ++i) {
527 const int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_index[node.id];
528 add_arcs_for_node(node, from_bucket, res_inc, local_arcs);
529 }
530 }
531 });
532
533 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
534 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
535}
pool

◆ get_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
AdjList BucketGraph::get_adjacency_list ( )
inline

Definition at line 1296 of file BucketGraph.h.

1296 {
1297 AdjList adjacency_list;
1298
1299 // Iterate through all nodes in the graph
1300 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1301 std::vector<std::tuple<int, double, int>> arcs;
1302
1303 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1304 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {
1305 int to_node = arc.to;
1306 double cost = getcij(node.id, to_node) - nodes[to_node].cost;
1307 int capacity = 1;
1308
1309 // Only add arcs with non-zero capacity
1310 if (capacity > 0) {
1311 arcs.emplace_back(to_node, cost, capacity);
1312 }
1313 }
1314
1315 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has
1316 // any outgoing edges
1317 if (!arcs.empty()) {
1318 adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs;
1319 }
1320 }
1321
1322 return adjacency_list;
1323 }

◆ get_best_label()

template<Direction D>
Label * BucketGraph::get_best_label ( const std::vector< int > & topological_order,
const std::vector< double > & c_bar,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > & sccs )

Retrieves the best label from the bucket graph based on the given topological order, c_bar values, and strongly connected components.

This function iterates through the given topological order and for each component, it retrieves the labels from the corresponding buckets in the bucket graph. It then compares the cost of each label and keeps track of the label with the lowest cost. The best label, along with its associated bucket, is returned.

Definition at line 549 of file BucketUtils.h.

551 {
552 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
553 Label *best_label = nullptr; // Ensure this is initialized
554 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
555
556 for (const int component_index : topological_order) {
557 const auto &component_buckets = sccs[component_index];
558
559 for (const int bucket : component_buckets) {
560 const auto &label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label();
561 if (!label) continue;
562 // print label->cost
563 if (label->cost < best_cost) {
564 best_cost = label->cost;
565 best_label = label;
566 }
567 }
568 }
569
570 return best_label;
571}

101 val = bw_node_interval_trees[node].query(resource_values_vec);
102 }

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 930 of file BucketUtils.h.

931 {
932 // TODO: adjust to multi-resource case
933 auto &current_bucket = (D == Direction::Forward)
934 ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index]
935 : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
936 int &node = current_bucket.node_id;
937 auto &theNode = nodes[node];
938
939 // Find the opposite bucket using the appropriate direction
940 int opposite_bucket_index = -1;
941 std::vector<double> reference_point(options.main_resources.size());
942 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
943 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
944 reference_point[r] = std::max(inc[r], theNode.lb[r]);
945 } else {
946 reference_point[r] = std::min(inc[r], theNode.ub[r]);
947 }
948 }
949 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
950 opposite_bucket_index =
951 get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
952 } else {
953 opposite_bucket_index =
954 get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
955 }
956
957 return opposite_bucket_index;
958}

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

1034 {
1035 reset_pool();
1036 reset_fixed();
1037 common_initialization();
1038
1039 // Pre-allocate vectors with reserve to avoid reallocations
1040 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(),
1041 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1042 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(),
1043 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1044
1045 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::One, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar,
1046 backward_cbar);
1047
1048 // Pre-allocate with exact sizes
1049 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
1050 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> fw_labels_map(num_nodes);
1051 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> bw_labels_map(num_nodes);
1052
1053 // Pre-reserve space based on average bucket size
1054 size_t avg_bucket_size = 0;
1055 for (const auto &bucket : fw_buckets) {
1056 avg_bucket_size += bucket.get_labels().size();
1057 }
1058 avg_bucket_size /= fw_buckets.size();
1059
1060 for (auto &labels : fw_labels_map) labels.reserve(avg_bucket_size);
1061 for (auto &labels : bw_labels_map) labels.reserve(avg_bucket_size);
1062
1063 // Optimized group_labels with direct indexing
1064 auto group_labels = [](const auto &buckets, auto &labels_map) {
1065 for (const auto &bucket : buckets) {
1066 const auto &bucket_labels = bucket.get_labels();
1067 for (auto *label : bucket_labels) {
1068 labels_map[label->node_id].push_back(label);
1069 }
1070 }
1071 };
1072
1073 // Parallel execution of grouping
1074 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1075 group_labels(fw_buckets, fw_labels_map);
1076 });
1077 auto backward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1078 group_labels(bw_buckets, bw_labels_map);
1079 });
1080
1081 stdexec::sync_wait(
1082 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)));
1083
1084 // Pre-compute minimum cost labels for each node
1085 std::vector<const Label *> min_fw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1086 std::vector<const Label *> min_bw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1087
1088 auto compute_min_labels = [](const auto &labels_map, auto &min_labels) {
1089 for (size_t i = 0; i < labels_map.size(); ++i) {
1090 const auto &labels = labels_map[i];
1091 if (!labels.empty()) {
1092 min_labels[i] =
1093 *std::min_element(labels.begin(), labels.end(),
1094 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) {
1095 return a->cost < b->cost;
1096 });
1097 }
1098 }
1099 };
1100
1101 // Parallel computation of minimum labels
1102 auto min_fw_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1103 compute_min_labels(fw_labels_map, min_fw_labels);
1104 });
1105 auto min_bw_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1106 compute_min_labels(bw_labels_map, min_bw_labels);
1107 });
1108
1109 stdexec::sync_wait(
1110 stdexec::when_all(std::move(min_fw_task), std::move(min_bw_task)));
1111
1112 // Pre-compute resource indices
1113 const size_t time_index = std::distance(
1114 options.resources.begin(),
1115 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time"));
1116
1117 size_t num_fixes = 0;
1118 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1119 const auto *min_fw_label = min_fw_labels[i];
1120 if (!min_fw_label) continue;
1121
1122 const VRPNode &L_last_node = nodes[min_fw_label->node_id];
1123
1124 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
1125 if (i == j) continue;
1126
1127 const auto *min_bw_label = min_bw_labels[j];
1128 if (!min_bw_label) continue;
1129
1130 const double cost =
1131 getcij(min_fw_label->node_id, min_bw_label->node_id);
1132
1133 // Check time resource first as it's often the most restrictive
1134 if (options.resources[time_index] == "time") {
1135 if (min_fw_label->resources[time_index] + cost +
1136 L_last_node.duration >
1137 min_bw_label->resources[time_index]) {
1138 continue;
1139 }
1140 }
1141
1142 // Check other resources
1143 bool violated = false;
1144 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1145 if (r != time_index) {
1146 if (min_fw_label->resources[r] +
1147 L_last_node.consumption[r] >
1148 min_bw_label->resources[r]) {
1149 violated = true;
1150 break;
1151 }
1152 }
1153 }
1154
1155 if (!violated &&
1156 min_fw_label->cost + cost + min_bw_label->cost > gap) {
1157 // fixed_arcs[i][j] = 1;
1158 fix_arc(i, j);
1159 ++num_fixes;
1160 }
1161 }
1162 }
1163}

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 877 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

877 {
878 // Print header
879 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
880 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
881 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
882
883 // Print Resource size
884 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
885 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
886
887 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
888#ifdef RIH
889 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
890#else
891 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
892#endif
893#ifdef RCC
894 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
895#else
896 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
897#endif
898#ifdef SRC
899 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
900#else
901 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
902#endif
903 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
904
905 fmt::print("\n");
906}

◆ is_dominated() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::is_dominated ( const Label *__restrict new_label,
const Label *__restrict label )
inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

Definition at line 849 of file BucketSolve.h.

850 {
851 // Early exit on cost check
852 const double cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
853 if (cost_diff > 0) {
854 return false;
855 }
856
857 // Resource comparison using vectorization-friendly code
858 const auto *__restrict new_resources = new_label->resources.data();
859 const auto *__restrict label_resources = label->resources.data();
860
861 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
862 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
863 // In Forward direction: the comparison label must not have more
864 // resources than the new label
865 if (label_resources[i] > new_resources[i]) {
866 return false;
867 }
868 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
869 // In Backward direction: the comparison label must not have
870 // fewer resources than the new label
871 if (label_resources[i] < new_resources[i]) {
872 return false;
873 }
874 }
875 }
876
877 // Check visited nodes (bitmap comparison) for Stages 3, 4, and
878 // Enumerate
879 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
880 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
881 // Iterate through the visited bitmap and ensure that the new label
882 // visits all nodes that the comparison label visits
883 for (size_t i = 0; i < label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
884 // If the comparison label visits a node that the new label does
885 // not, it is not dominated
886 if (((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) ^
887 label->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
888 return false;
889 }
890 }
891 }
892
893#ifdef SRC
894 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
895 const auto *__restrict label_srcs = label->SRCmap.data();
896 const auto *__restrict new_label_srcs = new_label->SRCmap.data();
897
898 // Process active cuts in batches for better cache utilization
899 double sum = 0.0;
900 const auto &active_cuts = cut_storage->getActiveCuts();
901 const size_t num_cuts = active_cuts.size();
902
903 // Process cuts in batches
904 for (auto &cut : active_cuts) {
905 if (label_srcs[cut.index] > new_label_srcs[cut.index]) {
906 sum += cut.dual_value;
907 if (cost_diff - sum > 0) {
908 return false;
909 }
910 }
911 }
912 }
913#endif
914
915 return true;
916}

◆ is_node_unreachable()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

Definition at line 583 of file BucketGraph.h.

584 {
585 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment
586 // contains the node_id
587 int bit_position =
588 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment
589 return (bitmap[word_index] & (1ULL << bit_position)) != 0;
590 }

◆ is_node_visited()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

237 // Get references to avoid repeated lookups
