240 auto &topological_order =
241 assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
242 auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
243 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
244 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
245 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
246 auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
247 auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
248 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
249 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
250 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
251 auto &best_label = assign_buckets<D>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
252
253 n_labels = 0;
254
255 // Pre-calculate segment info using bit shift for better performance
256 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
257 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments);
258
259 // Process SCCs in topological order
260 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
261 bool all_ext;
262 do {
263 all_ext = true;
264
265 // Process buckets in sorted order within SCC
266 for (const auto bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
267 // Cache bucket labels reference
268 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_labels();
269
270 for (Label *label : bucket_labels) {
271 if (label->is_extended || label->is_dominated) continue;
272
273 // Early resource check for partial solutions
274 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
275 if constexpr (F == Full::Partial) {
276 const auto main_resource =
277 label->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
278 const auto q_star_value =
279 q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
280
281 if (((D == Direction::Forward) &&
282 (main_resource > q_star_value)) ||
283 ((D == Direction::Backward) &&
284 (main_resource <= q_star_value))) {
285 label->set_extended(true);
286 continue;
287 }
288 }
289 }
290
291 // Efficient visited bucket clearing
292 if (n_segments <= 8) {
293 std::fill_n(Bvisited.begin(), n_segments, 0);
294 } else {
295 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
296 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
297 }
298
299 // Check dominance in smaller buckets
300 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
301 // random change of callong the function
302 // auto change = rand() % 100;
303 // if (change < 95) {
304 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(
305 label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, stat_n_dom)) {
306 // buckets[label->vertex].lazy_flush(label);
307 // label->set_extended(true);
308 label->set_dominated(true);
309 continue;
310 // }
311 }
312 }
313
314 // Process arcs for current label
315 auto node_id = label->node_id;
316
317 const auto &node_arcs =
318 nodes[node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
319
320 for (const auto &arc : node_arcs) {
321 auto extended_labels =
322 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Node, Mutability::Mut, F>(
323 label, arc);
324 if (extended_labels.empty()) {
325#ifdef UNREACHABLE_DOMINANCE
326 set_node_unreachable(label->unreachable_bitmap,
327 arc.to);
328#endif
329 continue;
330 }
331 arc_scores[node_id][arc] += extended_labels.size();
332
333 // Process each new label
334 for (Label *new_label : extended_labels) {
335 ++stat_n_labels;
336
337 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
338 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
339 const auto &to_bucket_labels =
340 mother_bucket.get_labels();
341
342 bool dominated = false;
343 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
344 // Stage One processing with early exits
345 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
346 if (label->cost < existing_label->cost) {
347 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
348 } else {
349 dominated = true;
350 break;
351 }
352 }
353 } else {
354 // SIMD dominance check
355 auto check_dominance_in_bucket =
356 [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels,
357 int &stat_n_dom) {
358#ifndef __AVX2__
359 if (labels.size() >= 32) {
360 return check_dominance_against_vector<
361 D, S>(new_label, labels,
362 cut_storage,
363 options.resources.size(),
364 stat_n_dom);
365 } else
366#endif
367 {
368 for (auto *existing_label :
369 labels) {
370 if (existing_label
371 ->is_dominated) {
372 continue;
373 }
374 if (is_dominated<D, S>(
375 new_label,
376 existing_label)) {
377 ++stat_n_dom;
378 return true;
379 }
380 }
381 return false;
382 }
383 };
384
385 dominated = mother_bucket.check_dominance(
386 new_label, check_dominance_in_bucket,
387 stat_n_dom);
388 }
389
390 if (!dominated) {
391 // if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
392 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
393 if (existing_label->is_dominated) {
394 continue;
395 }
396 if (is_dominated<D, S>(existing_label,
397 new_label)) {
398 // buckets[to_bucket].remove_label(
399 // existing_label);
400 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
401 }
402 }
403 // }
404
405 ++n_labels;
406#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
407 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
408#elif LIMITED_BUCKETS
409 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label,
410 BUCKET_CAPACITY);
411#else
412 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
413#endif
414 all_ext = false;
415 }
416 }
417 }
418 label->set_extended(true);
419 }
420 // buckets[bucket].flush();
421 }
422 } while (!all_ext);
423
424 // Update c_bar values efficiently
425 for (const int bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
426 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
427 for (const auto phi_bucket : Phi[bucket]) {
428 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_bucket]);
429 }
430 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
431 }
432 }
433
434 // Store best label
435 if (Label *best_label_run =
436 get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs)) {
437 best_label = best_label_run;
438 }
439
440 return c_bar;
441}

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

570 {
571 // Clear previous data
572 merged_labels.clear();
573 merged_labels.reserve(100);
574 fw_c_bar.clear();
575 bw_c_bar.clear();
576
577 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
578 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
579
580 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
581 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
582 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
583
584 auto &num_buckets =
585 assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
586 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(
587 num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
588
589 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
590 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
591 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
592
593 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
594
595 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
596 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
597 base_intervals[r] =
598 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
599 }
600
601 // Clear forward and backward buckets
602 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
603 fw_buckets[b].clear();
604 bw_buckets[b].clear();
605 }
606
607 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
608 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
609
610 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
611 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
612
613 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
614 // bounds
615 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
616 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
617 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
618 }
619 int offset_fw = 0;
620 int offset_bw = 0;
621
622 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
623 // combinations
624 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
625 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
626 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
627 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
628 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
629 // print depot_id
630 int calculated_index_base =
631 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
632 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
633
634 std::vector<int> current_pos(
635 num_intervals,
636 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
637 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
638
639 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
640 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
641 if (depth == num_intervals) {
642 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
643 // combination
644 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
645 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
646
647 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval
648 // starts or ends
649 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
650 interval_bounds[r] =
651 is_forward
652 ? interval_starts[r] +
653 current_pos[r] *
654 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
655 : interval_ends[r] -
656 current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(
657 base_intervals[r]);
658 }
659
660 // print interval
661 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
662 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
663 depot_id);
664 depot->is_extended = false;
665 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
666 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
667 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
668 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
669 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
670 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
671 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
672
673 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
674 return;
675 }
676
677 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
678 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
679 current_pos[depth] = k;
680 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
681 }
682 };
683
684 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
685 generate_combinations(0);
686 };
687
688 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring
689 // all combinations are processed
690 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
691}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 340 of file BucketJump.h.

340 {
341 // Assign forward or backward buckets, fixed buckets, bucket indices, and
342 // Phi (adjacency list) based on direction
343 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
344 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
345 auto &num_buckets_index =
346 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
347 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
348 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
349 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
350
351 int arc_counter = 0; // Counter for jump arcs added
352 int missing_counter = 0; // Counter for missing paths
353
354 // iterate over all nodes and clear jump arcs
355 for (auto &node : nodes) {
356 node.clear_jump_arcs<D>();
357 }
358
359 // Loop through all buckets
360 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
361 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid bucket indices
362
363 // Cache the current bucket's node ID and arcs
364 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
365 const auto &arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
366 const auto &original_arcs =
367 nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
368
369 if (arcs.empty()) {
370 continue;
371 } // Skip if no arcs in the current bucket
372
373 // Process each original arc for the current node
374 for (const auto &orig_arc : original_arcs) {
375 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
376 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
377 bool have_path = false;
378
379 // Check if the path exists in the current bucket arcs
380 for (const auto &gamma : arcs) {
381 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) {
382 continue;
383 }
384
385 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
386 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
387
388 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
389 have_path = true;
390 break; // Path found, exit early
391 }
392 }
393
394 // If no path was found, find a jump arc
395 if (!have_path) {
396 ++missing_counter; // Increment missing path counter
397
398 // Cache the starting bucket and the number of buckets for this
399 // node
400 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_index[current_node_id];
401 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
402
403 auto process_bucket = [&](int b, int b_prime) {
404 // Check if b_prime is adjacent to b in Phi
405 const auto &phi_b_prime = Phi[b_prime];
406 if (std::find(phi_b_prime.begin(), phi_b_prime.end(), b) ==
407 phi_b_prime.end()) {
408 return; // Not adjacent, skip
409 }
410
411 // Check arcs in the adjacent bucket b_prime
412 const auto &arcs_prime =
413 buckets[b_prime].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
414 for (const auto &gamma_prime : arcs_prime) {
415 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_prime.from_bucket,
416 gamma_prime.to_bucket)) {
417 continue;
418 }
419
420 const int from_node_prime =
421 buckets[gamma_prime.from_bucket].node_id;
422 const int to_node_prime =
423 buckets[gamma_prime.to_bucket].node_id;
424
425 // If the arc matches, add a jump arc
426 if (from_node == from_node_prime &&
427 to_node == to_node_prime) {
428 B_bar.push_back(
429 b_prime); // Add the valid bucket to B_bar
430
431 // Wrap the resource increment in a vector
432 const std::vector<double> res =
433 gamma_prime.resource_increment;
434 const double cost = gamma_prime.cost_increment;
435
436 buckets[b].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, b_prime, res,
437 cost);
438 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template add_jump_arc<D>(
439 b, b_prime, res, cost, to_node);
440 ++arc_counter; // Increment the jump arc counter
441 }
442 }
443 };
444
445 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
446 // Forward direction: incrementing buckets
447 for (int b_prime = b + 1;
448 b_prime < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++b_prime) {
449 process_bucket(b, b_prime);
450 }
451 } else {
452 // Backward direction: decrementing buckets
453 // for (int b_prime = b - 1; b_prime >= start_bucket;
454 // --b_prime) { process_bucket(b, b_prime); }
455 for (int b_prime = b + 1;
456 b_prime < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++b_prime) {
457 process_bucket(b, b_prime);
458 }
459 }
460 }
461 }
462 }
463 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
464 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
465}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 728 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

728 {
729 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
730 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
731 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
732
733 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
734 fmt::print(
735 "\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
736 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset,
737 " Forward", " Backward");
738 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
739
740 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
741 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset,
742 stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
743
744 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
745 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold,
746 " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
747
748 // Print the final horizontal line
749 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
750
751 fmt::print("\n");
752}
reset
constexpr auto reset

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 106 of file BucketRes.h.

109 {
110 for (size_t I = 0; I < N; ++I) {
111 switch (static_cast<ResourceType>(options.resource_type[I])) {
112 [[likely]] case ResourceType::Disposable:
113 if (!process_disposable_resource<D>(
114 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
115 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.lb[I],
116 theNode.ub[I])) {
117 return false;
118 }
119 break;
120
121 case ResourceType::NonDisposable:
122 if (!process_non_disposable_resource<D>(
123 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
124 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.lb[I],
125 theNode.ub[I])) {
126 return false;
127 }
128 break;
129
130 case ResourceType::Binary:
131 new_resources[I] =
132 process_binary_resource<D>(gamma.resource_increment[I]);
133 break;
134
135 case ResourceType::Battery:
136 if (!process_battery_resource<D>(
137 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
138 theNode.is_station, options.battery_capacity)) {
139 return false;
140 }
141 break;
142
143 case ResourceType::RechargeCount:
144 if (!process_recharge_count<D>(
145 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
146 theNode.is_station, options.max_recharges)) {
147 return false;
148 }
149 break;
150
151 case ResourceType::RechargeTime:
152 if (!process_recharge_time<D>(
153 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
154 theNode.is_station, options.max_recharge_time)) {
155 return false;
156 }
157 break;
158
159 case ResourceType::MTW:
160 if (!process_mtw_resource<D>(
161 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
162 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode)) {
163 return false;
164 }
165 break;
166 }
167 }
168 return true;
169}

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

void BucketGraph::redefine ( int bucketInterval)
inline

Redefines the bucket intervals and reinitializes various data structures.

This function updates the bucket interval and reinitializes the intervals, buckets, fixed arcs, and fixed buckets. It also generates arcs and sorts them for each node.

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

◆ reset_pool()

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1054 of file BucketSolve.h.

1055 {
1056 // Create tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms
1057
1058 auto forward_task =
1059 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1060 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1061 });
1062
1063 auto backward_task =
1064 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1065 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1066 });
1067
1068 // Execute the tasks in parallel and synchronize
1069 auto work =
1070 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1071 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1072 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1073 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1074 });
1075
1076 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
1077 // forward_cbar = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state,
1078 // fullness>(); backward_cbar = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward,
1079 // state, fullness>();
1080 //
1081 // #pragma omp parallel sections
1082 // {
1083 // #pragma omp section
1084 // {
1085 // forward_cbar =
1086 // labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state,
1087 // fullness>();
1088 // }
1089 // #pragma omp section
1090 // {
1091 // backward_cbar =
1092 // labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state,
1093 // fullness>();
1094 // }
1095 // }

◆ SCC_handler()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::SCC_handler ( )

Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.

This function processes the bucket graph to identify SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm. It extends the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets, computes the SCCs, and orders them topologically. It also sorts the buckets within each SCC based on their lower or upper bounds, depending on the direction. Finally, it splits the arcs for each SCC and removes duplicates.

Definition at line 716 of file BucketUtils.h.

716 {
717 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
718 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
719 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
720 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph =
721 bucket_graph;
722
723 // Extend the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets
724 for (auto i = 0; i < extended_bucket_graph.size(); ++i) {
725 auto phi_set = Phi[i];
726 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
727 for (auto &phi_bucket : phi_set) {
728 extended_bucket_graph[phi_bucket].push_back(i);
729 }
730 }
731
732 SCC scc_finder;
733 scc_finder.convertFromUnorderedMap(
734 extended_bucket_graph); // print extended bucket graph
735
736 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
737 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
738
739#ifdef VERBOSE
740 // print SCCs and buckets in it
741 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
742 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
743 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
744 fmt::print("FW SCCs: \n");
745 } else {
746 fmt::print("BW SCCs: \n");
747 }
748 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
749 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
750 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
751 } else {
752 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
753 }
754 for (auto &bucket : sccs[scc]) {
755 fmt::print("{} ", bucket);
756 }
757 }
758 fmt::print("\n");
759#endif
760
761 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
762 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size()); // Reserve space for all SCCs
763 for (int i : topological_order) {
764 ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[i]);
765 }
766
767 auto sorted_sccs = sccs;
768 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
769 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
770 std::sort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
771 return buckets[a].lb[0] < buckets[b].lb[0];
772 });
773 } else {
774 std::sort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
775 return buckets[a].ub[0] > buckets[b].ub[0];
776 });
777 }
778 }
779
780 // iterate over nodes
781 for (auto &node : nodes) {
782 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
783 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
784 } else {
785 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
786 }
787 }
788
789 // Split arcs for each SCC
790 auto scc_ctr = 0;
791 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
792 // Iterate over each bucket in the SCC
793 for (int bucket : scc) {
794 int from_node_id =
795 buckets[bucket].node_id; // Get the source node ID
796 VRPNode &node =
797 nodes[from_node_id]; // Access the corresponding node
798 // Define filtered arcs depending on the direction
799 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
800 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_fw_arcs =
801 nodes[from_node_id]
802 .fw_arcs_scc[scc_ctr]; // For forward direction
803
804 // Iterate over the arcs from the current bucket
805 const auto &bucket_arcs =
806 buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
807 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
808 int to_node_id =
809 buckets[arc.to_bucket]
810 .node_id; // Get the destination node ID
811
812 // Search for the arc from `from_node_id` to `to_node_id` in
813 // the node's arcs
814 auto it =
815 std::find_if(node.fw_arcs.begin(), node.fw_arcs.end(),
816 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) {
817 return a.to == to_node_id;
818 });
819
820 // If both nodes are within the current SCC, retain the arc
821 if (it != node.fw_arcs.end()) {
822 // Add the arc to the filtered arcs
823 filtered_fw_arcs.push_back(*it); // Forward arcs
824 }
825 }
826 } else {
827 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_bw_arcs =
828 nodes[from_node_id]
829 .bw_arcs_scc[scc_ctr]; // For forward direction
830
831 // Iterate over the arcs from the current bucket
832 const auto &bucket_arcs =
833 buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
834 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
835 int to_node_id =
836 buckets[arc.to_bucket]
837 .node_id; // Get the destination node ID
838
839 // Search for the arc from `from_node_id` to `to_node_id` in
840 // the node's arcs
841 auto it =
842 std::find_if(node.bw_arcs.begin(), node.bw_arcs.end(),
843 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) {
844 return a.to == to_node_id;
845 });
846
847 // If both nodes are within the current SCC, retain the arc
848 if (it != node.bw_arcs.end()) {
849 // Add the arc to the filtered arcs
850 filtered_bw_arcs.push_back(*it); // Forward arcs
851 }
852 }
853 }
854 }
855
856 // Increment SCC counter
857 ++scc_ctr;
858 }
859
860 for (auto &node : nodes) {
861 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
862 // Iterate over all SCCs for each node
863 for (auto &fw_arcs_scc : node.fw_arcs_scc) {
864 // Sort arcs by from_bucket and to_bucket
865 std::sort(fw_arcs_scc.begin(), fw_arcs_scc.end(),
866 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
867 return std::tie(a.from, a.to) <
868 std::tie(b.from, b.to);
869 });
870
871 // Remove consecutive duplicates
872 auto last =
873 std::unique(fw_arcs_scc.begin(), fw_arcs_scc.end(),
874 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
875 return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to;
876 });
877
878 // Erase the duplicates from the vector
879 fw_arcs_scc.erase(last, fw_arcs_scc.end());
880 }
881 } else {
882 // Iterate over all SCCs for each node
883 for (auto &bw_arcs_scc : node.bw_arcs_scc) {
884 // Sort arcs by from_bucket and to_bucket
885 std::sort(bw_arcs_scc.begin(), bw_arcs_scc.end(),
886 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
887 return std::tie(a.from, a.to) <
888 std::tie(b.from, b.to);
889 });
890
891 // Remove consecutive duplicates
892 auto last =
893 std::unique(bw_arcs_scc.begin(), bw_arcs_scc.end(),
894 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
895 return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to;
896 });
897
898 // Erase the duplicates from the vector
899 bw_arcs_scc.erase(last, bw_arcs_scc.end());
900 }
901 }
902 }
903
904 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
905 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
906 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
907 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
908 fw_sccs = sccs;
909 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
910 fw_union_find = uf;
911 } else {
912 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
913 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
914 bw_sccs = sccs;
915 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
916 bw_union_find = uf;
917 }
918}

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 1166 of file BucketUtils.h.

1166 {
1167 // Clear existing arcs
1168 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1169 node.clear_arcs();
1170 }
1171
1172 // Compute clusters using MST-based clustering
1173 MST mst_solver(nodes,
1174 [this](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
1175
1176 // Compute theta dynamically based on the distribution of edge weights
1177 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
1178 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
1179 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
1180 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) {
1181 edge_weights.push_back(weight);
1182 }
1183
1184 // Sort edge weights to compute a dynamic percentile
1185 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
1186 double theta = edge_weights[static_cast<size_t>(0.9 * edge_weights.size())];
1187 theta = theta / 100;
1188
1189 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
1190
1191 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
1192 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
1193
1194 // Create job-to-cluster mapping
1195 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
1196 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
1197 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
1198 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
1199 }
1200 }
1201
1202 // Pre-calculate time resource index
1203 const int time_resource_idx =
1204 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time") -
1205 options.resources.begin();
1206
1207 // Lambda for processing node arcs
1208 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
1209 std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
1210 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
1211 std::vector<Arc> forward_arcs;
1212 std::vector<Arc> reverse_arcs;
1213 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1214 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1215
1216 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
1217 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id ||
1218 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot ||
1219 next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
1220 continue;
1221 }
1222
1223 const auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
1224 const double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
1225 const int to_bucket = next_node.id;
1226
1227 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) continue;
1228
1229 // Calculate resource increments
1230 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
1231 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1232 res_inc[r] = r == time_resource_idx
1233 ? travel_cost + node.duration
1234 : node.consumption[r];
1235 }
1236 } else {
1237 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1238 res_inc[r] =
1239 r == time_resource_idx
1240 ? node.duration / 2 + travel_cost +
1241 next_node.duration / 2
1242 : (node.consumption[r] + next_node.consumption[r]) /
1243 2;
1244 }
1245 }
1246
1247 // Check resource feasibility
1248 bool feasible = true;
1249 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1250 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
1251 feasible = false;
1252 break;
1253 }
1254 }
1255 if (!feasible) continue;
1256
1257 // Calculate priorities
1258 const bool same_cluster =
1259 job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id];
1260 const double base_priority = same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0;
1261 const double priority =
1262 base_priority + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time;
1263 const double rev_priority =
1264 (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) + 1.E-5 * node.start_time;
1265
1266 forward_arcs.emplace_back(priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1267 cost_inc);
1268 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(rev_priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1269 cost_inc);
1270 }
1271
1272 // Add forward arcs
1273 for (const auto &[priority, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] :
1274 forward_arcs) {
1275 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
1276 node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc, priority);
1277 }
1278
1279 // Add reverse arcs
1280 for (const auto &[priority, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] :
1281 reverse_arcs) {
1282 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
1283 to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc, priority);
1284 }
1285 };
1286
1287 // Process all nodes
1288 std::vector<double> res_inc(intervals.size());
1289 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1290 if (node.id != options.end_depot) {
1291 add_arcs_for_node(node, node.id, res_inc);
1292 }
1293 }
1294}

433 {
434 // Clear existing arcs for each node

440 // Step 1: Compute the clusters using MST-based clustering
441 MST mst_solver(nodes,
442 [&](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
443 double theta = 1.0; // Experiment with different values of θ
444 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
445
446 // Create a job-to-cluster mapping (cluster ID for each job/node)
447 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(
448 nodes.size(), -1); // Mapping from job (node) to cluster ID
449 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
450 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
451 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
452 }
453 }
454
455 // Step 2: Modify add_arcs_for_node to give priority based on cluster
456 // membership
457 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
458 std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
459 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>,
460 double>; // Arc: priority, to_node, resource
461 // increments, cost increment
462
463 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs;
464 best_arcs.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for forward arcs
465
466 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs_rev;
467 best_arcs_rev.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for reverse arcs
468
469 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
470 if (!manual_arcs.has_arc(node.id, next_node.id))
471 continue; // Skip arcs not in the manual list
472
473 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id)
474 continue; // Skip depot and same node
475
476 auto travel_cost =
477 getcij(node.id, next_node.id); // Calculate travel cost
478 double cost_inc =
479 travel_cost - next_node.cost; // Adjust cost increment by
480 // subtracting next node's cost
481
482 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
483 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
484 res_inc[r] = travel_cost +
485 node.duration; // Update resource increment
486 // based on node duration
487 } else {
488 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
489 }
490 }
491 // res_inc[TIME_INDEX] += travel_cost; // Add travel time to
492 // resource increment
493
494 int to_bucket = next_node.id;
495 if (from_bucket == to_bucket)
496 continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
497
498 bool feasible =
499 true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints
500 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
501 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
502 feasible = false;
503 break;
504 }
505 }
506 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
507
508 // Step 3: Calculate priority based on cluster membership
509 double priority_value;
510 double reverse_priority_value;
511 if (job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]) {
512 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster
513 priority_value =
514 5.0 +
515 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
516 // same-cluster priority
517 reverse_priority_value =
518 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
519 // same-cluster priority
520 } else {
521 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
522 priority_value =
523 1.0 +
524 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for
525 // cross-cluster arcs
526 reverse_priority_value =
527 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for
528 // cross-cluster arcs
529 }
530
531 best_arcs.emplace_back(priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
532 cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
533 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id,
534 res_inc,
535 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
536 }
537
538 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
539 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
540 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
541 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
542 node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
543 priority_value); // Add forward arc
544 }
545
546 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node
547 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs_rev) {
548 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
549 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
550 to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
551 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
552 }
553 };
554
555 // Step 4: Iterate over all nodes to set the adjacency list
556 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
557 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot)
558 continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
559
560 std::vector<double> res_inc(
561 intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
562 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id,
563 res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
564 }
565}

Sets the distance matrix and calculates neighborhoods.

Marks a node as unreachable in the given bitmap.

This function sets the bit corresponding to the specified node_id in the bitmap, indicating that the node is unreachable.

◆ shallUpdateStep()

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ solve()

template<Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solve ( bool trigger = false)
inline

Solves the bucket graph optimization problem using a multi-stage bi-labeling algorithm.

The function iteratively adjusts the terminal time and handles different stages of the bi-labeling algorithm to find the optimal paths. The stages are adaptive and transition based on the inner objective values and iteration counts.

59 transition = true;
60 fixed = false;

64
65 // Placeholder for the final paths (labels) and inner objective value
66 std::vector<Label *> paths;
67

71 // Stage 1: Apply a light heuristic (Stage::One)
72 if (s1 && depth == 0) {
73 stage = 1;
74 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>(); // Solve the problem with

98 }
99 }
100 // Stage 3: Apply a heuristic fixing approach (Stage::Three)

102 stage = 3;
103 paths =
104 bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Three>(); // Solve the problem with
105 // Stage 3 heuristic
106 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
107
108 // Transition from Stage 3 to Stage 4 if the objective improves

110 if (inner_obj >= -0.5) {
111 s4 = true;
112 s3 = false;
113 transition = true; // Prepare for transition to Stage 4
114 }
115 }
116 // Stage 4: Exact labeling algorithm with fixing enabled (Stage::Four)
117 else if (s4 && depth == 0) {
118 stage = 4;
119
120#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
121 // If transitioning to Stage 4, print a message and apply fixing
122 if (transition) {
123 // print_cut("Transitioning to stage 4\n");
124 bool original_fixed = fixed; // Store the original fixed status
125 fixed = true; // Enable fixing of buckets in Stage 4
126 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>(); // Solve the problem
127 // with Stage 4
128 transition = false; // End the transition period
129 fixed = original_fixed; // Restore the original fixed status
130 min_red_cost = paths[0]->cost; // Update the minimum reduced cost
131 iter++;
132 return paths; // Return the final paths
133 }
134#endif
135 // If not transitioning, continue with Stage 4 algorithm
136 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

Definition at line 24 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

24 {
25
26 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
27
28 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
29 std::vector<double> all_costs;
30 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
31
32 // Mutex for shared resources
33 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
34
35 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
36 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
37 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
38
39 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
40 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
41 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
42 if (i == j) continue;
43 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
44 }
45 }
46 auto bulk_sender =
47 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
48 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
49 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
50 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
51 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
52 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
53 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
54 BucketOptions sub_options;
55 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
56 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
57 sub_options.depot = i;
58 sub_options.end_depot = j;
59 // print i and j
60 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
61 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
62 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
63 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
64 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
65 sub_bg.setup();
66 sub_bg.reset_pool();
67 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
68 // Solve and get the paths
69 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
70 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
71 std::vector<Label *> paths;
72 // print paths.size
73 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
74 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
75 // print bucket_labels size
76 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
77 if (!label) continue;
78 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
79 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
80 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
81 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
82 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
83 paths.push_back(new_label);
84 }
85 }
86 }
87
88 // Check if no paths are found
89 if (paths.empty()) return;
90
91 // Lock to protect shared resources
92 {
93 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
94 // Process the found paths
95 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
96 }
97 // }
98 }
99 });
100
101 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
102 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
103 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
104
105 return all_paths;
106}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP_by_MTZ ( )

356 {
357 int JOBS = nodes.size();
358
359 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
360 std::vector<double> all_costs;
361 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
362 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
363
364 auto duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, true);
365 auto max_pstep_iterations = 10;
366 for (auto z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
367 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
368 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
369 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
370 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
371 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
372 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
373
374 // Convert three_two to vector of pairs
375 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
376 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
377 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
378 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
379 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
380
381 // Convert three_three to vector of pairs
382 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
383 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
384 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
385 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
386 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
387
388 // Convert three_five to vector of pairs of pairs
389 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
390 size_t total_size = 0;
391 for (const auto &row : three_five_duals) total_size += row.size();
392 arc_duals_tuples.reserve(total_size);
393
394 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
395 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
396 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
397 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
398 }
399 }
400 }
401 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
402
403 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
404 // print sub_paths.size
405 auto paths_added = 0;
406 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
407 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
408 paths_added++;
409 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
410 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
411 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
412 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
413 }
414 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
415
416 duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix);
417 }
418 return {};
419}

auto BucketGraph::solveTSP ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 228 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

230 {
231 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
232 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsp", 0, 0);
233 if (first_time) { generateInitialPaths(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix); }
234 // Define variables
235 int R = paths.size(); // Number of p-steps
236 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for each p-step
237 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> u(n); // Continuous variables for each node
238
239 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
240 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
241 // fmt::print("path_costs[{}] = {}\n", i, path_costs[i]);
242 std::string varname = "x_" + std::to_string(i);
243 double custo = path_costs[i];
244 x[i] = model.add_variable(varname, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1.0, custo);
245 }
246
247 // Create continuous variables u_i for each node i
248 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
249 auto name = "u_" + std::to_string(i);
250 u[i] = model.add_variable(name, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, n - 1, 0.0);
251 }
252 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
253
254 // Constraints (3.3) and (3.4):
255 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
256 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
257 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
258 LinearExpression in_constraint;
259 LinearExpression out_constraint;
260 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
261 if (firsts[r] == i) { in_constraint += x[r]; }
262 if (lasts[r] == i) { in_constraint -= x[r]; }
263 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end() && i != lasts[r]) {
264 out_constraint += x[r];
265 }
266 }
267 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(in_constraint == 0, "33_" + std::to_string(i));
268 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(out_constraint == 1, "34_" + std::to_string(i));
269 }
270
271 // Constraints (3.5): Avoid sub-tours using MTZ constraints
272 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
273 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
274 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
275 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
276 if (i != j) {
277 LinearExpression r_sum;
278 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
279 for (int k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
280 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { r_sum += x[r]; }
281 }
282 }
283 auto ctr = u[i] - u[j] + (n - 1) * r_sum <= (n - 2);
284 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] =
285 model.add_constraint(ctr, "mtz_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
286 }
287 }
288 }
289
290 for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
291 model.add_constraint(u[i] >= 0, "u_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
292 model.add_constraint(u[i] <= n - 1, "u_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
293 }
294
295#ifdef IPM
296 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
297 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
298 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
299
300 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
301#else
302 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
303#endif
304 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
305 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
306 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
307 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
308 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
309 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
310 }
311
312 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
313 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
314 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
315 if (i != j) {
316 three_five_duals[i][j] =
317 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
318 }
319 }
320 }
321#ifdef IPM
322 auto lp_obj = ipSolver->getObjective();
323#else
324 auto lp_obj = 0.0;
325#endif
326 fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", lp_obj);
327 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
328 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release(); // Release ownership from unique_ptr
329 // set model verbose
330 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 0);
331 // set model type as 2
332 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 2);
333
334 // convert x vars to int and solve again
335 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
336 modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).set(GRB_CharAttr_VType, GRB_BINARY);
337 }
338 modelGRB->update();
339 modelGRB->optimize();
340
341 if (modelGRB->get(GRB_IntAttr_Status) == GRB_OPTIMAL) {
342 fmt::print("Integer solution found with value: {}\n", modelGRB->get(GRB_DoubleAttr_ObjVal));
343 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
344 if (modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X) > 0.5) {
345 fmt::print("x_{} = {}\n", i, modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X));
346 // print path nodes
347 for (auto node : paths[i]) { fmt::print("{} ", node); }
348 fmt::print("\n");
349 }
350 }
351 }
352
353 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
354}

IPSolver::run_optimization
void run_optimization(ModelData &model, const double tol)
Runs the optimization process on the given model data.

◆ solveTSPTW()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSPTW ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
std::vector< double > & service_times,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_start,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_end,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 421 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

425 {
426 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
427 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsptw", 0, 0);
428
429 if (first_time) {
430 generateInitialPathsTSPTW(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix, time_windows_start,
431 time_windows_end, service_times);
432 }
433
434 // Variables
435 int R = paths.size();
436 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for p-steps
437 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> omega(n); // Continuous variables for accumulated time
438
439 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
440 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
441 x[i] = model.add_variable("x_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Binary, 0.0, 1.0, path_costs[i]);
442 }
443
444 // Create continuous variables ω_i for time accumulation
445 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
446 omega[i] = model.add_variable("omega_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1e8, 0.0);
447 }
448 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
449
450 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
451 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
452 // Constraints (4.11): Flow balance for p-steps
453 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
454 LinearExpression flow_balance;
455 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
456 if (firsts[r] == i) flow_balance += x[r];
457 if (lasts[r] == i) flow_balance -= x[r];
458 }
459 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(flow_balance == 0, "flow_" + std::to_string(i));
460 }
461
462 // Constraints (4.12): Visit each node at most once
463 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
464 LinearExpression visit_once;
465 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
466 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { visit_once += x[r]; }
467 }
468 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(visit_once <= 1, "visit_" + std::to_string(i));
469 }
470
471 // Constraint (4.13): Visit source exactly once
472 LinearExpression source_visit;
473 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
474 if (firsts[r] == 0) { source_visit += x[r]; }
475 }
476 model.add_constraint(source_visit == 1, "source");
477
478 // Constraints (4.14): Time accumulation with big-M
479 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
480 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
481 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
482 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
483 if (i != j) {
484 double M_ij = 1e8;
485 LinearExpression time_acc;
486 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
487 for (size_t k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
488 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { time_acc += x[r]; }
489 }
490 }
491 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] = model.add_constraint(
492 omega[j] - omega[i] - (cost_matrix[i][j] + service_times[i]) * time_acc - M_ij * time_acc >= -M_ij,
493 "time_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
494 }
495 }
496 }
497
498 // define omega[0] = 0
499
500 // Constraints (4.15): Time window constraints
501 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
502 LinearExpression node_used;
503 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
504 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { node_used += x[r]; }
505 }
506 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_start[i] * node_used >= 0, "tw_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
507 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_end[i] * node_used <= 0, "tw_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
508 }
509
510// Solve the model
511#ifdef IPM
512 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
513 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
514 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
515
516 // Extract solution and duals
517 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
518#else
519 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
520#endif
521 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
522 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
523 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
524 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
525 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
526 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
527 fmt::print("Duals for node {}: 3.2 = {}, 3.3 = {}\n", i, three_two_duals[i], three_three_duals[i]);
528 }
529
530 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
531 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
532 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
533 if (i != j) {
534 three_five_duals[i][j] =
535 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
536 }
537 }
538 }
539
540 // Solution output and return
541 // fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", ipSolver->getObjective());
542
543 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
544
545 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release();
546 // set model verbose
547 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 1);
548 // set model type as 2
549 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 0);
550
551 modelGRB->update();
552 modelGRB->optimize();
553
554 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
555}
generateInitialPathsTSPTW
void generateInitialPathsTSPTW(int n, std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, const std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix, const std::vector< double > &start_times, const std::vector< double > &end_times, const std::vector< double > &service_times)

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solveTSPTW_by_MTZ ( )

Definition at line 557 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

557 {
558 int JOBS = nodes.size();
559
560 // Initialize containers for storing paths, costs, firsts, and lasts
561 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
562 std::vector<double> all_costs;
563 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
564 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
565
566 // extract service time from nodes
567 std::vector<double> service_times;
568 std::vector<double> time_windows_start(JOBS, 0.0);
569 std::vector<double> time_windows_end(JOBS, 10000.0);
570 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS; ++i) {
571 time_windows_start[i] = nodes[i].lb[0];
572 time_windows_end[i] = nodes[i].ub[0];
573 service_times.push_back(nodes[i].duration);
574 }
575 // Step 1: Solve the initial TSPTW using the previously defined method
576 auto duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
577 time_windows_start, time_windows_end, true);
578
579 // Step 2: Set up iteration for generating new paths using dual values
580 int max_pstep_iterations = 10;
581 for (int z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
582 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
583 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
584
585 // Extract dual values for constraints
586 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
587 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
588 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
589 // auto arrival_time_duals = std::get<3>(duals);
590
591 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
592
593 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.32)
594 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
595 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
596 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
597 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
598 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
599
600 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.33)
601 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
602 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
603 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
604 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
605 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
606
607 // Convert dual values for MTZ constraints (3.34) and time windows (3.35)
608 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
609 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
610 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
611 if (i != j) { arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]); }
612 }
613 }
614 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
615
616 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
617 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
618 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
619 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
620 }
621 }
622 }
623 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
624
625 // Step 3: Solve the subproblem to generate new p-steps
626 fmt::print("Solving PSTEP subproblem\n");
627 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
628 fmt::print("Subproblem solved\n");
629
630 // Add the new paths to the existing list if valid
631 int paths_added = 0;
632 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
633 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
634 paths_added++;
635 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
636 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
637 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
638 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
639 }
640
641 // If no new paths were added, stop the iteration
642 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
643
644 // Step 4: Re-solve the TSPTW with the updated set of p-steps
645 duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
646 time_windows_start, time_windows_end);
647 }
648
649 return {};
650}

auto solveTSPTW(std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix, std::vector< double > &service_times, std::vector< double > &time_windows_start, std::vector< double > &time_windows_end, bool first_time=false)

void BucketGraph::split_buckets ( )

676 {
677 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
678 auto &buckets_size =
679 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
680 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
681 auto &num_buckets_index =
682 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
683
684 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
685 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
686 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
687
688 // Determine the base interval and other relevant values for
689 // each resource
690 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
691 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
692 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
693 }
694
695 int original_num_buckets = buckets_size;
696
697 // To track splits per node_id
698 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, int> splits_per_node;
699
700 // Loop until the second-to-last bucket to avoid
701 // out-of-bounds access
702 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets - 1; ++i) {
703 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
704
705 // Calculate mid-point for each dimension
706 std::vector<double> mid_point(bucket.lb.size());
707 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
708 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) {
709 mid_point[r] = (bucket.lb[r] + base_intervals[r]) / 2.0;
710 }
711 } else {
712 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) {
713 mid_point[r] = (bucket.ub[r] - base_intervals[r]) / 2.0;
714 }
715 }
716
717 // Create two new buckets by splitting at the mid-point
718 Bucket bucket1, bucket2;
719 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
720 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
721 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, mid_point, bucket.ub);
722 } else {
723 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
724 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, mid_point);
725 }
726
727 // Manually shift elements to the right to make space
728 // for the new bucket
729 for (int j = original_num_buckets; j > i + 1; --j) {
730 buckets[j] = buckets[j - 1];
731 }
732
733 // Insert the new bucket at position i+1
734 buckets[i + 1] = bucket2;
735
736 // Replace the current bucket with the first half
737 // (bucket1)
738 buckets[i] = bucket1;
739
740 // Track the number of splits for this node_id
741 splits_per_node[bucket.node_id]++;
742
743 // Since we added a new bucket at i+1, increment i to
744 // skip over the new bucket
745 ++i;
746 ++original_num_buckets; // Adjust the count to account
747 // for the new bucket
748 }
749
750 // Update the global bucket size
751 buckets_size = original_num_buckets;
752
753 // Now update the num_buckets_index for each node_id based
754 // on the final positions
755 int current_index = 0;
756 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets; ++i) {
757 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
758 if (num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] == -1) {
759 num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] = current_index;
760 }
761 current_index++;
762 }
763 }

◆ UpdateBucketsSet()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::UpdateBucketsSet ( double theta,
const Label * label,
ankerl::unordered_dense::set< int > & Bbidi,
int & current_bucket,
std::vector< uint64_t > & Bvisited )

Updates the set of buckets based on the given parameters.

This function inserts the current bucket into the visited set and then performs various checks and updates based on the provided theta, label, Bbidi, current_bucket, and Bvisited parameters.

Definition at line 41 of file BucketJump.h.

44 {
45 // Precompute values and assign references for the direction-specific data
46
47 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
48 auto &buckets_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_buckets, fw_buckets);
49 auto &c_bar_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_c_bar, fw_c_bar);
50
51 const int bucketLnode = label->node_id;
52 std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
53 bucket_stack.reserve(10); // Pre-allocate space for the stack
54 bucket_stack.push_back(current_bucket);
55
56 // Lambda to compare bitmaps
57 auto bitmaps_conflict = [&](const Label *L1, const Label *L2) {
58 for (size_t i = 0; i < L1->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
59 if ((L1->visited_bitmap[i] & L2->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
60 return true;
61 }
62 }
63 return false;
64 };
65
66 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
67 int curr_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
68 bucket_stack.pop_back();
69 const size_t segment =
70 curr_bucket >> 6; // Determine the segment for the current bucket
71 const size_t bit_position =
72 curr_bucket & 63; // Determine the bit position within the segment
73 Bvisited[segment] |=
74 (1ULL << bit_position); // Set the bit corresponding to the current
75 // bucket as visited
76 // print current bucket
77 const int bucketLprimenode = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].node_id;
78 double cost = getcij(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
79
80 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(auto arc_dual =
81 arc_duals.getDual(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
82 cost -= arc_dual;)
83 // Stop exploring if the cost exceeds the threshold
84 if (label->cost + cost + c_bar_opposite[curr_bucket] >= theta) {
85 continue;
86 }
87
88 // Only process if bucket is not already in Bbidi
89 if (Bbidi.insert(curr_bucket).second) {
90 const auto opposite_labels =
91 buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].get_labels();
92 for (const auto &L : opposite_labels) {
93 if (label->node_id == L->node_id ||
94 bitmaps_conflict(label, L)) {
95 continue; // Skip labels with the same node ID or
96 // conflicting bitmaps
97 }
98
99 const VRPNode &L_last_node = (D == Direction::Forward)
100 ? nodes[label->node_id]
101 : nodes[L->node_id];
102
103 bool violated = false;
104 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
105 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
106 const double time_constraint =
107 (D == Direction::Forward)
108 ? label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost +
109 L_last_node.duration
110 : L->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost +
111 L_last_node.duration;
112
113 if (time_constraint >
114 ((D == Direction::Forward)
115 ? L->resources[TIME_INDEX]
116 : label->resources[TIME_INDEX])) {
117 violated = true;
118 break;
119 }
120 } else {
121 const double resource_constraint =
122 (D == Direction::Forward)
123 ? label->resources[r] +
124 L_last_node.consumption[r]
125 : L->resources[r] + L_last_node.consumption[r];
126
127 if (resource_constraint > ((D == Direction::Forward)
128 ? L->resources[r]
129 : label->resources[r])) {
130 violated = true;
131 break;
132 }
133 }
134 }
135
136 if (violated) {
137 continue;
138 }
139
140 // Insert the current bucket into Bbidi if the total cost is
141 // below theta
142 if (label->cost + cost + L->cost < theta) {
143 Bbidi.emplace(curr_bucket);
144 }
145 }
146 }
147
148 // Add neighboring buckets to the stack, only if not visited
149 for (int b_prime : Phi_opposite[curr_bucket]) {
150 const size_t segment_prime =
151 b_prime >>
152 6; // Determine the segment for the neighboring bucket
153 const size_t bit_position_prime =
154 b_prime & 63; // Determine the bit position within the segment
155
156 // If the neighboring bucket hasn't been visited, push it onto the
157 // stack
158 if ((Bvisited[segment_prime] & (1ULL << bit_position_prime)) == 0) {
159 bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
160 }
161 }
162 }
163}

◆ updateSplit()

void BucketGraph::updateSplit ( )
inline

Definition at line 879 of file BucketGraph.h.

879 {
880 const double BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD =
881 0.15; // Slightly lower base threshold
882 const double MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.02;
883 const double MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.10;
884 const double LEARNING_RATE_DECAY =
885 0.99; // Decay factor for learning rate
886 const double EMA_ALPHA = 0.1; // Smoothing factor for EMA
887
888 static double learning_rate =
889 MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR; // Initialize learning rate
890 static double ema_imbalance_ratio =
891 0.0; // Initialize EMA of imbalance ratio
892
893 // Calculate label counts and ratios
894 double fw_labels = static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels);
895 double bw_labels = static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels);
896 double total_labels = fw_labels + bw_labels;
897
898 // Skip if we don't have enough labels for meaningful
899 // adjustment
900 if (total_labels < 10) return;
901
902 // Calculate imbalance ratio
903 double fw_ratio = fw_labels / total_labels;
904 double bw_ratio = bw_labels / total_labels;
905 double imbalance_ratio = std::abs(fw_ratio - bw_ratio);
906
907 // Update EMA of imbalance ratio
908 ema_imbalance_ratio =
909 EMA_ALPHA * imbalance_ratio + (1 - EMA_ALPHA) * ema_imbalance_ratio;
910
911 // Dynamic threshold based on total number of labels
912 // As we get more labels, we can be more sensitive to
913 // imbalance
914 double dynamic_threshold =
915 BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD * std::exp(-total_labels / 1000.0);
916
917 if (ema_imbalance_ratio > dynamic_threshold) {
918 // Calculate adaptive adjustment factor
919 // More severe imbalance = larger adjustment
920 double severity = (ema_imbalance_ratio - dynamic_threshold) /
921 (1.0 - dynamic_threshold);
922 double adjustment_factor =
923 MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR +
924 (MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR - MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR) * severity;
925
926 // Adjust learning rate based on the severity of
927 // imbalance
928 learning_rate *= LEARNING_RATE_DECAY;
929
930 // Adjust based on resource range
931 double resource_range = R_max[options.main_resources[0]] -
932 R_min[options.main_resources[0]];
933 double base_adjustment =
934 adjustment_factor * resource_range * learning_rate;
935
936 // Scale adjustment based on current split position
937 for (size_t i = 0; i < q_star.size(); ++i) {
938 double relative_pos =
939 (q_star[i] - R_min[options.main_resources[0]]) /
940 resource_range;
941
942 // Smaller adjustments near boundaries
943 double position_factor =
944 4.0 * relative_pos * (1.0 - relative_pos);
945
946 // Determine direction and apply adjustment
947 double direction = (bw_labels > fw_labels) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
948 double final_adjustment =
949 base_adjustment * position_factor * direction;
950
951 // Apply adjustment with boundary protection
952 q_star[i] = std::clamp(q_star[i] + final_adjustment,
953 R_min[options.main_resources[0]] +
954 resource_range * 0.1, // Lower bound
955 R_max[options.main_resources[0]] -
956 resource_range * 0.1 // Upper bound
957 );
958 }
959
960 if (std::abs(base_adjustment) > resource_range * 0.05) {
961 print_info(
962 "Split adjustment: imbalance={:.3f}, "
963 "adjustment={:.3f}, "
964 "learning_rate={:.3f}\n",
965 ema_imbalance_ratio, base_adjustment, learning_rate);
966 }
967 }
968 }
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ updateStepSize()

bool BucketGraph::updateStepSize ( )
inline

Definition at line 972 of file BucketGraph.h.

972 {
973 bool updated = false;
974 if (bucket_interval >= 100) {
975 return false;
976 }
977 // compute the mean of dominance_checks_per_bucket
978 double mean_dominance_checks = 0.0;
979 for (size_t i = 0; i < dominance_checks_per_bucket.size(); ++i) {
980 mean_dominance_checks += dominance_checks_per_bucket[i];
981 }
982 auto step_calc =
983 mean_dominance_checks / non_dominated_labels_per_bucket;
984 if (step_calc > 100) {
985 // redefine_counter = 0;
986 print_info("Increasing bucket interval to {}\n",
987 bucket_interval + 25);
988 bucket_interval = bucket_interval + 25;
989 redefine(bucket_interval);
990 updated = true;
991 fixed = false;
992 }
993 return updated;
994 }
